opportunity_overview.title: National Recruitment Advertising Campaign Services (NRAC)
opportunity_overview.notice_type: Solicitation (Request for Proposal - RFP); Combined Synopsis/Solicitation for Commercial Items
opportunity_overview.agency: United States Secret Service (USSS), Department of Homeland Security (DHS)
opportunity_overview.office: Office of Human Resources (HUM), Recruitment Division (RECDIV)
opportunity_overview.response_deadline: DISCREPANCY BETWEEN DOCUMENTS: Original Synopsis states 4:00 PM EDT, April 28, 2026. Amendment 1 states 4:00 PM EDT, May 8, 2026. THE AMENDMENT 1 DEADLINE OF MAY 8, 2026 IS THE CONTROLLING DATE.
source_documents: Attch 1 NRAC Performance Work Statement.pdf - PWS defining all technical requirements, security, and deliverables
Attch 2 Provisons and Clauses.pdf - FAR/HSAR provisions and contract clauses
Attch 4 Direct Labor Matrix.xlsx - Pricing template with five labor categories across five ordering years
Attch 5 Sample Task Order SOW.pdf - Sample Task Order with specific impression and applicant targets
Combined Synopsis Solicitation 70US0926R70093666.pdf - Original combined synopsis/solicitation
Combined Synopsis Solicitation 70US0926R70093666 Amend 1.pdf - Amendment 1 superseding original submission deadline and Q&A dates
US_Secret Service_compliance_matrix.docx - Section L/M compliance matrix cross-referencing PWS requirements

contract_details.contract_type: Firm Fixed-Price (FFP) Indefinite Delivery/Indefinite Quantity (IDIQ) - single award; task orders issued under IDIQ on FFP basis per PWS Section 1.3.6. NOTE: PWS acronym list also references CPFF, LH, and T&M contract types, but the awarded vehicle is explicitly stated as FFP IDIQ.
contract_details.set_aside: Total Small Business Set-Aside (FAR 52.219-6, Notice of Total Small Business Set-Aside, incorporated by reference in Attachment 2; FAR 52.219-14 Limitations on Subcontracting also applies)
contract_details.estimated_value: IDIQ ceiling: $90,000,000.00 (total contract ceiling across all task orders). Minimum guarantee: $1,000.00. Per FAR 52.216-19 Order Limitations, maximum single task order: $30,000,000. No single base/option year breakdown specified; ordering period runs July 1, 2026 through June 30, 2031 with a potential 6-month extension under FAR 52.217-8 (through approximately December 31, 2031). FAR 52.216-22 states no deliveries required after July 31, 2031.
contract_details.period_of_performance: Base ordering period: July 1, 2026 – June 30, 2031 (five-year ordering period per PWS Section 1.2.8 and Synopsis). FAR 52.217-8 Option to Extend Services: up to 6 additional months beyond the ordering period (not to exceed total of 6 months). FAR 52.216-22 bars deliveries after July 31, 2031. Task orders may be issued July 1, 2026 through June 30, 2030 per FAR 52.216-18 Ordering clause. NOTE DISCREPANCY: FAR 52.216-18 states orders may be issued 'from July 1, 2026, through June 30, 2030' (4-year ordering window), while the Synopsis and PWS state a 5-year ordering period through June 30, 2031. BD TEAM SHOULD SEEK CLARIFICATION via Q&A.
contract_details.place_of_performance: Primarily at contractor's facility. Intermittent access required at: U.S. Secret Service Memorial Headquarters Building, Washington, D.C.; James J. Rowley Training Center, Laurel, MD; and other USSS-controlled facilities and/or venues as determined by RECDIV. Contractor also required to travel CONUS and within the National Capital Region (NCR) for meetings, conferences, and training.

requirements_summary.scope_overview: The USSS requires a full-service advertising contractor to plan, design, produce, execute, evaluate, and measure a National Recruitment Advertising Campaign (NRAC) across all media formats to support national, regional, and local recruiting efforts for Special Agents, Uniformed Division Officers, and Administrative/Professional/Technical positions.
key_requirements: Multi-channel advertising and marketing: TV, radio, streaming video/audio, social media (LinkedIn, YouTube, Twitter), print (magazine, newspaper), outdoor/transit, direct mail, internet, academia outreach; prime-time ad placement targeting audiences of 10,000+ per campaign
Programmatic active job board advertising: Capability to reach 70,000+ applicants per year via Indeed, LinkedIn, Clearance Jobs, Zip Recruiter, and similar platforms

Sample Task Order targets: 41,250 new LEO applicants (52% of agency goal) via active job channels in 12 months; 84 million streaming video impressions/year; 140 million streaming audio impressions/year; 25 million YouTube pre-roll impressions/year
Recruiting events: Plan and assist USSS with nationwide special recruiting events attracting qualified candidates; propose integrated advertising and special events campaigns
Hosting: Coordinate and procure (when government space unavailable) venue space for Entry Level Assessment Centers (ELACs) with required signage and branding

Recruiter sales training: Annual classroom training for 16+ RECDIV personnel; certify participants as trainers; training must deliver repeatable, proven sales process (rapport-building, objection-handling, commitment-closing)

Electronic tracking: Provide talent management technology (e.g., Yello or similar) for event management, candidate pipeline tracking from 'hello to application,' and mobile device data collection (iPad or similar) at career fairs
Multimedia/digital: Google G4 analytics or similar reporting; alignment guidance for secretservice.gov/careers website; creation of landing pages for promotional/special events
Promotional materials and supplies: USSS-branded merchandise (brochures, cups, pens, calendars, bags, flash drives) for distribution at recruitment events
Strategic planning and documentation: Written advertising plans, operational procedures, planning documents, meeting minutes, and status reports
Pre- and post-advertising research and tracking: Qualitative and quantitative research, surveys, and media tracking reports delivered per task order requirements
Quality Control Plan (QCP): Draft within 15 business days of award; final within 5 business days after government comments; must include badge/key control and lock combination procedures
All media produced becomes sole property of USSS; Government holds unlimited data rights including software source codes per PWS Section 1.3.13
Post-award conference attendance required per FAR Subpart 42.5
Electronic payment via Invoice Processing Platform (IPP); vendor registration information required at proposal submission
requirements_summary.clearance_requirements: No traditional security clearance (Secret/Top Secret) explicitly required. However: (1) All contractor employees and subcontractors must be U.S. citizens. (2) Suitability Assessment required via USSS Office of Security, Personnel Vetting Division (OSC-PSV) prior to work at USSS controlled facilities. (3) Low Risk positions require minimum Tier 1/NACI background investigation within last 5 years. (4) High Risk positions require minimum Tier 4 Public Trust background investigation within last 5 years and submission of DHS-11000-25, SSF 4024 (NDA), SSF 3230A, and OF 306. (5) Continuous Suitability Reviews required at minimum every two years. NOTE: PWS Section 1.3.7.2 contains placeholder text '[Insert level of clearance]' for a Facility Clearance from DCSA, suggesting a classified facility clearance may be required but was not finalized in the provided documents - BD TEAM SHOULD FLAG AND CLARIFY.
requirements_summary.key_personnel: Key Personnel identified in PWS Section 1.3.10 and defined per HSAR 3052.215-70. Key Personnel are identified at the Task Order level. Three required roles with minimum qualifications: (1) Project Leader - minimum 4-year degree (preferably Project Management focus), 5+ years experience in recruitment and advertising/marketing; (2) Market Analyst - minimum 4-year degree (preferably marketing, business administration, statistics, economics, or communications), 3+ years experience with complex analysis and independent project management in recruitment/advertising/marketing; (3) Marketing Coordinator - minimum 4-year degree (preferably marketing, communications, business administration, or public relations), 3+ years experience in marketing coordination. Contract Manager/alternate must be designated in writing to the CO.
requirements_summary.deliverables: Key deliverables include: (1) Quality Control Plan (QCP) - draft within 15 business days of award, final within 5 business days after government comments, submitted via email to CO and COR in PDF format; (2) Annual advertising and marketing plan; (3) Monthly reports based on research, target audience opinions, media tracking, and qualitative/quantitative studies (per task order); (4) Pre- and post-advertising research and tracking reports; (5) Written advertising plans, operational procedures, planning documents, meeting minutes, and status reports; (6) Post Award Conference attendance; (7) Sample Task Order: 12-month marketing plan following task order issuance; (8) Performance standards tracked quarterly (99% customer satisfaction rate; no more than 3 strategy deficiencies annually; no more than 3 responsiveness deficiencies annually)

submission_requirements.page_limit_total: 18 pages total for scored factors (Factor I: 12 pages + Factor II: 6 pages); Factor III has no page limit

page_limits_by_volume.Factor I - Technical Capability: 12 single-sided pages (excluding cover page and table of contents)
page_limits_by_volume.Factor II - Past Performance: 6 single-sided pages (excluding cover page and table of contents)
page_limits_by_volume.Factor III - Price: No page limit specified; must include pricing matrix, QCP/Post Award Conference pricing, and sample task order pricing

submission_requirements.font: Times New Roman, 10-point font or larger
submission_requirements.file_format: Factors I and II: Microsoft Word (.docx). Factor III: Microsoft Excel (.xlsx); Price Proposal Excel worksheet must be unlocked with formulas. Standard paper size 8.5 x 11 inches. 1-inch margins on all sides. Headers/footers must include offeror's name, solicitation number, and page number.
submission_requirements.copies: Not specified (electronic submission only; no hard copy requirement stated)
submission_requirements.submission_method: Email only - to both: John Akin, Contracting Specialist at john.akin@usss.dhs.gov AND Contracting Officer Erik Syfert at erik.syfert@usss.dhs.gov. Subject line must include solicitation number (70US0926R70093666) and offeror's name. NOT via SAM.gov portal.

volume_structure: Factor I - Technical Capability (Microsoft Word .docx, 12 pages max excl. cover/TOC)
Factor II - Past Performance (Microsoft Word .docx, 6 pages max excl. cover/TOC)

Factor III - Price/Pricing Matrix (Microsoft Excel .xlsx, no page limit; must include: (a) labor category unit prices for base year, each ordering year, and 6-month FAR 52.217-8 extension; (b) QCP and Post Award Conference pricing including all labor, material, travel, and ODCs; (c) sample task order pricing including all labor, material, travel, and ODCs; Excel must be unlocked with formulas)
section_l.signing_requirements: Offers must be submitted signed and dated per FAR 52.212-1(a). Offeror must provide UEI, TIN, and CAGE code. Must have active SAM registration at time of submission and award (FAR 52.204-7). Required certifications include: 52.209-2 (Inverted Domestic Corporations), 52.209-5 (Responsibility Matters), 52.209-11 (Delinquent Tax/Felony), 52.240-90 (Security Prohibitions). IPP registration information (Company Name, Vendor IPP Administrator POC Name and Email, W-9 with DUNS and TIN, ACH Form) must be submitted to ippvendors@usss.dhs.gov upon proposal submission.
section_l.other_instructions: Government intends to award without discussions - initial proposal should represent offeror's best terms. File naming convention required: '[Offeror Name] [Volume Number] [Volume Title]'. Questions deadline: April 17, 2026, 4:00 PM EDT (same in both original and Amendment 1). Q&A responses published on SAM.gov: DISCREPANCY - Original states April 21, 2026; Amendment 1 states April 29, 2026 (Amendment 1 controls). Non-compliance with any submission requirement may result in immediate elimination. Government may reject unreasonably high or unrealistically low pricing. 90-day price hold required from proposal submission date. OCI disclosure obligations apply. Non-U.S. citizens may not access DHS IT systems without waiver.

section_m.evaluation_method: Best Value Tradeoff (FAR Part 15). All non-price factors (Technical Capability and Past Performance) are of equal importance to each other. All non-price factors combined are more important than price. Government intends to award without discussions.

factors.name: Factor I - Technical Capability
factors.weight: Equal to Past Performance; combined non-price factors more important than price. Most important single criterion within Technical Capability: Active Job Boards with programmatic advertising.
subfactors: Proficiency in using various channels (social media, job boards, networking events, etc.) to attract candidates; most importantly, Active Job Boards with programmatic advertising
Innovation in sourcing techniques to reach active and passive candidates
Comprehension of the Law Enforcement Officer (LEO) industry, culture, and specific LEO job requirements
Capability to reach 70,000+ applicants per year with appropriate budget via Indeed, LinkedIn, Clearance Jobs, Zip Recruiter, and industry marketing techniques
Ability to provide valuable insights to USSS regarding talent acquisition and anticipated market shifts
Ability to adapt recruitment strategies flexibly and tailor hiring approaches for all USSS roles, including rapid scaling of specific roles

factors.name: Factor II - Past Performance
factors.weight: Equal to Technical Capability; combined non-price factors more important than price
subfactors: Recency: current or previous work within last 5 years of solicitation issue date
Relevancy: work similar in scope, size, and complexity to SOW; larger scope considered more relevant

factors.name: Factor III - Price
factors.weight: Less important than all non-price factors combined; evaluated but not scored/rated
subfactors: Reasonableness of proposed labor category unit prices and asset prices across IDIQ ordering period
Price realism (proposals unrealistically low may be rejected)
Sample task order pricing: reasonableness, realism, consistency with proposed rates, labor mix, level of effort, materials, and ODCs
QCP and Post Award Conference pricing including all labor, material, travel, and ODCs

section_m.notes: Technical Capability evaluated using Confidence Ratings (High Confidence / Some Confidence / Low Confidence). Past Performance evaluated using Performance Risk ratings (Low / Moderate / High / Unknown). Offerors with no recent and relevant past performance receive 'Unknown/Neutral' rating - neither advantaged nor disadvantaged, but Government may determine lower performance risk is worth more than Neutral in tradeoff. Government may check CPARS, SAM, reference checks, articles, and publications. Price proposals receive no adjectival rating. Failure to price all labor categories for entire ordering period AND FAR 52.217-8 extension may result in elimination.

competitive_intelligence.incumbent: Not specified in any provided document
competitive_intelligence.predecessor_contract: Not specified in any provided document
competitive_intelligence.estimated_competition: Total Small Business Set-Aside under NAICS 541810 (Advertising Agencies). This is a specialized niche requiring demonstrated ability to reach 70,000+ law enforcement applicants per year with programmatic job advertising at scale, plus multi-million dollar media buying capability across streaming, audio, and digital platforms - suggesting a relatively narrow field of small business advertising firms with federal/law enforcement recruitment experience. Single award IDIQ means only one firm wins.
competitive_intelligence.notable_terms: 1. PLACEHOLDER TEXT IN SECURITY SECTION: PWS Section 1.3.7.2 contains unfilled brackets '[Insert level of clearance]' suggesting a facility clearance requirement was not finalized - this is a material ambiguity. 2. SINGLE AWARD IDIQ: Only one contract will be awarded from this solicitation - all-or-nothing competition. 3. IPP REGISTRATION: Vendors must submit IPP enrollment information at time of proposal submission (not just at award) to ippvendors@usss.dhs.gov - failure to do so could be a compliance issue. 4. ALL MEDIA BECOMES GOVERNMENT PROPERTY: Unlimited data rights including software source codes - significant IP consideration for offerors. 5. UNAUTHORIZED USE OF USSS NAME: 18 U.S.C. 709 restriction on use of 'Secret Service' name without written Director permission - affects how contractor can reference the work in past performance/marketing. 6. MINIMUM GUARANTEE EXTREMELY LOW: $1,000 minimum guarantee on a $90M ceiling IDIQ represents significant risk that few or no task orders may be issued. 7. FAR 52.217-8 PRICING MANDATORY: Failure to price the 6-month extension option will result in elimination. 8. SERVICE CONTRACT LABOR STANDARDS APPLY: FAR 52.222-41 applies - offerors must account for wage determinations in pricing. 9. FASCSA/TELECOM PROHIBITIONS: FAR 52.240-91 prohibits Huawei, ZTE, Hytera, Hikvision, Dahua, Kaspersky, TikTok/ByteDance products - relevant for any tech/software solutions proposed.

bd_action_items.pursuit_recommendation: Best positioned firm: A small business advertising agency (NAICS 541810 compliant) with demonstrated federal or law enforcement/government recruitment advertising experience, active programmatic job advertising capability at scale (Indeed, LinkedIn, programmatic platforms), and proven media buying/placement track record across streaming video, streaming audio, and digital channels. Firms with prior USSS, DHS, DoD, or other federal law enforcement agency recruitment campaign experience are strongly advantaged on past performance. The 70,000+ applicants/year and $90M ceiling suggest this is a sizable, complex campaign requiring both advertising sophistication and federal compliance depth.

April 21, 2026, 4:00 PM EDT - Q&A responses published on SAM.gov (ORIGINAL SOLICITATION - SUPERSEDED by Amendment 1)
April 28, 2026, 4:00 PM EDT - Proposal submission deadline (ORIGINAL SOLICITATION - SUPERSEDED by Amendment 1)
April 29, 2026, 4:00 PM EDT - Q&A responses published on SAM.gov (AMENDMENT 1 - CONTROLLING DATE)
May 8, 2026, 4:00 PM EDT - Proposal submission deadline (AMENDMENT 1 - CONTROLLING DATE)
July 1, 2026 - Anticipated contract start / first day of ordering period
June 30, 2030 - Last day task orders may be issued per FAR 52.216-18 (NOTE DISCREPANCY WITH 5-YEAR ORDERING PERIOD)
June 30, 2031 - Anticipated end of ordering period per Synopsis and PWS

risks_and_flags: MINIMUM GUARANTEE RISK: $1,000 minimum guarantee on a $90M ceiling IDIQ is extremely low - the government has minimal obligation to fund work. Offerors should understand this is essentially a contract vehicle with no guaranteed revenue.

UNFILLED SECURITY PLACEHOLDER: PWS Section 1.3.7.2 contains '[Insert level of clearance]' brackets that were never filled in. This creates ambiguity about whether a DCSA facility clearance is required. BD team must submit Q&A to clarify before proposal submission.

ORDERING PERIOD DISCREPANCY: FAR 52.216-18 (Ordering clause in Attachment 2) states task orders may be issued 'from July 1, 2026, through June 30, 2030' (4 years), while the Synopsis/PWS states a 5-year ordering period through June 30, 2031. This is a material discrepancy that must be resolved via Q&A.
ALL-OR-NOTHING SINGLE AWARD: This is a single-award IDIQ. Losing firms receive nothing. Bid/no-bid decision should reflect high stakes of this competition.

USSS NAME USE RESTRICTION (18 U.S.C. 709): Contractor cannot use 'Secret Service' branding without written Director permission. This directly affects how the contractor markets its own past performance and limits the firm's ability to publicly reference this work.

IP/DATA RIGHTS: All media produced - including software source codes - becomes sole government property. Firms with proprietary methodologies, creative assets, or platforms should carefully evaluate this requirement.

SERVICE CONTRACT LABOR STANDARDS: FAR 52.222-41 applies. Wage determinations will affect labor pricing. No wage determination number or specific area WD was included in the provided documents - this gap needs to be addressed.

SAMPLE TASK ORDER PRICING IS EVALUABLE: The sample task order pricing (84M streaming video impressions, 140M streaming audio impressions, 25M YouTube pre-roll impressions) will be evaluated for reasonableness and realism against the offeror's proposed rates. Firms need to price this competitively and consistently.
CITIZENSHIP REQUIREMENT FOR ALL PERSONNEL: All contractor employees AND subcontractors must be U.S. citizens. This limits teaming/subcontracting options.
IPP REGISTRATION AT PROPOSAL SUBMISSION: Vendors must submit IPP enrollment information at the time of proposal submission (not just at award). Many firms may overlook this requirement.
FAR 52.217-8 PRICING MANDATORY: Failure to price the 6-month extension option for each labor category is an explicit elimination criterion.
FUNDS NOT AVAILABLE AT AWARD: FAR 52.232-18 (Availability of Funds) is included - contract may be awarded before funds are appropriated. No legal liability exists until funds are made available.
cross_document_discrepancies: PROPOSAL DEADLINE DISCREPANCY: Original Synopsis states deadline of April 28, 2026, 4:00 PM EDT. Amendment 1 states May 8, 2026, 4:00 PM EDT. Amendment 1 controls - deadline is May 8, 2026.

Q&A RESPONSE DATE DISCREPANCY: Original Synopsis states Q&A responses will be published April 21, 2026 by 4:00 PM EDT. Amendment 1 states April 29, 2026 by 4:00 PM EDT. Amendment 1 controls - Q&A response date is April 29, 2026.

ORDERING PERIOD DISCREPANCY: Synopsis and PWS Section 1.2.8 state a five-year ordering period (July 1, 2026 – June 30, 2031). FAR 52.216-18 (Ordering clause in Attachment 2) states orders may be issued 'from July 1, 2026, through June 30, 2030' - a four-year window. This is a material inconsistency requiring Q&A clarification.

UNFILLED SECURITY CLEARANCE PLACEHOLDER: PWS Section 1.3.7.2 contains '[Insert level of clearance]' and '[Insert the attachment number]' placeholders that were never completed. No DD 254 attachment was included in the package. This creates ambiguity about whether a classified facility clearance is required - a potentially disqualifying requirement for firms without facility clearances.

QCP DELIVERY TIMELINE INCONSISTENCY: PWS Section 1.3.1 states draft QCP shall be delivered within '15 business days' after award. The Deliverables Schedule in PWS Part 4 states '15 calendar days.' These are materially different timeframes and require clarification.

CONTRACT TYPE ACRONYM LIST VS. STATED CONTRACT TYPE: PWS Section 2.2 (Acronyms) lists CPFF, LH, and T&M as defined contract types, suggesting multiple contract types may be in play. However, PWS Section 1.3.6 explicitly states 'The Government will award a firm fixed-price Indefinite Delivery/Indefinite Quantity contract.' The listed acronyms appear to be boilerplate and may not apply to this specific acquisition.

sections.title: Factor I - Technical Capability (12 pages max, Microsoft Word .docx)
requirements_addressed: Proficiency in programmatic active job board advertising (Indeed, LinkedIn, Clearance Jobs, Zip Recruiter) - MOST IMPORTANT subfactor per Section M
Capability to reach 70,000+ applicants per year with appropriate budget
Innovation in sourcing techniques for active and passive LEO candidates
Comprehension of LEO industry, culture, and specific job requirements
Multi-channel advertising approach: streaming video/audio, social media, print, outdoor, digital
Ability to provide talent acquisition insights and anticipate market shifts
Flexibility to rapidly scale recruitment for specific USSS roles
Sample Task Order deliverables: 84M streaming video impressions, 140M streaming audio impressions, 25M YouTube pre-roll impressions, 41,250 new LEO applicants
Talent management technology (Yello or similar), mobile device data collection
Google G4 analytics reporting, website alignment, landing page creation
Recruiter sales training program for 16+ RECDIV personnel
ELAC venue coordination and hosting capability
USSS-branded promotional materials and supplies
Quality Control Plan approach and performance measurement
Staffing plan addressing Key Personnel qualifications (Project Leader, Market Analyst, Marketing Coordinator)
Phase-in plan (30 days)
sections.guidance: Lead with a concrete, data-driven programmatic advertising methodology that explicitly demonstrates the ability to generate 70,000+ LEO applicants per year through active job boards, and thread LEO/law enforcement cultural intelligence throughout every element of the technical approach to maximize confidence ratings.

sections.title: Factor II - Past Performance (6 pages max, Microsoft Word .docx)
requirements_addressed: Recency: contracts within last 5 years of solicitation issue date
Relevancy: similar scope, size, and complexity to NRAC requirements (federal/government recruitment advertising preferred; law enforcement or DHS experience highly advantageous)
Quality of performance (tie to CPARS ratings if available)
Schedule adherence in prior campaigns
Management effectiveness and responsiveness
Scale of prior campaigns (larger scope considered more relevant per Section M)
sections.guidance: Present 2-3 highly relevant past performance examples emphasizing measurable outcomes (applicant volumes generated, impressions delivered, cost-per-applicant achieved) from federal or law enforcement recruitment campaigns, and proactively address any negative CPARS information with documented corrective actions.

sections.title: Factor III - Price Proposal (No page limit, Microsoft Excel .xlsx, unlocked with formulas)
requirements_addressed: Direct Labor Matrix: fully burdened labor rates for Project Leader, Market Analyst, and Marketing Coordinator for Years 1-5 and FAR 52.217-8 6-month extension
QCP and Post Award Conference pricing: all labor, materials, travel, and ODCs

Sample Task Order pricing: all labor, materials, media buys, travel, and ODCs consistent with Attachment 5 deliverables (active job channels, streaming video, streaming audio, YouTube pre-roll, 12-month marketing plan, monthly reports)
Pricing reasonableness, realism, balance, and completeness across entire ordering period
FAR 52.217-8 extension pricing for each labor category (failure to include is elimination criterion)
sections.guidance: Price the sample task order at market-competitive media rates that are internally consistent with proposed labor rates, demonstrating a realistic path to 84M streaming video impressions, 140M streaming audio impressions, and 25M YouTube pre-roll impressions within a credible budget that supports the 70,000+ applicant goal.

sections.title: IPP Enrollment Information (Submitted separately to ippvendors@usss.dhs.gov at time of proposal submission)
requirements_addressed: Company Name
Vendor IPP Administrator POC Name and Email
W-9 with current DUNS and TIN
ACH Form with current banking information
USSS Electronic Submission of Payment Requests requirement
sections.guidance: Treat this as a pass/fail compliance requirement and submit simultaneously with the proposal to avoid procedural elimination.

win_themes: PROVEN LEO APPLICANT GENERATION AT SCALE: Demonstrate a documented, data-backed methodology and track record for generating large volumes of qualified law enforcement applicants through programmatic job advertising and multi-channel digital campaigns, with specific metrics (cost-per-applicant, conversion rates, applicant volumes) that validate the ability to hit 70,000+ applicants per year for USSS.

FULL-SERVICE FEDERAL RECRUITMENT ADVERTISING EXPERTISE: Position the firm as a uniquely capable full-service partner that seamlessly integrates programmatic advertising, media buying, talent management technology, recruiter training, event execution, and government compliance (suitability, CUI, IPP, OCI) under a single contract vehicle - eliminating the coordination risk of managing multiple vendors.

AGILITY AND MEASURABILITY: Emphasize the ability to rapidly adapt campaign strategy, scale specific roles at a moment's notice, and deliver real-time analytics (Google G4, media tracking, candidate pipeline dashboards) that give USSS leadership continuous visibility into campaign ROI and enable data-driven course corrections throughout the 5-year ordering period.
